Magnolia dealbata, also known as Whitebay Magnolia, is a plant species within the Magnoliaceae family. It is a deciduous tree with beautiful white flowers that have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Magnolia dealbata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to have various biological activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Magnolia dealbata include tea, extracts, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
